perspective: Software engineer validating data-to-text correctness and deterministic behavior

criteria.description: Data-text consistency - if sleep=2h, text should reflect severe sleep deprivation

scoring_guide.1: Text completely contradicts the data (e.g. 'Great sleep!' when sleep=2h)
scoring_guide.2: Text understates or overstates the data significantly
scoring_guide.3: Text roughly matches data but misses severity or nuance
scoring_guide.4: Text accurately reflects data with one minor mismatch
scoring_guide.5: Text perfectly mirrors the severity and meaning of the underlying data

fail_examples: Sleep=2h: 'Solid night of rest, you're well recovered'
Steps=500: 'Great activity day, keep it up!'
Readiness=20: 'You're primed for a big workout'
pass_examples: Sleep=2h: 'Very low sleep last night - prioritize rest today'
Steps=500: 'Light movement day. Even a short walk adds up'
Readiness=20: 'Recovery is low. Take it easy and let your body reset'

criteria.description: Score-mode alignment - readiness score matches guidance mode (low score produces rest mode)

scoring_guide.1: Mode completely mismatches score (push mode at readiness=15)
scoring_guide.2: Mode is wrong by one level (moderate mode at readiness=15)
scoring_guide.3: Mode direction is correct but intensity is off
scoring_guide.4: Mode matches score with minor calibration issue
scoring_guide.5: Mode perfectly corresponds to the readiness score range

fail_examples: Readiness=15, mode=push: 'Time to go all out today!'
Readiness=90, mode=rest: 'Take a full rest day'
Readiness=30, mode=moderate: 'A solid moderate effort is great today'
pass_examples: Readiness=15, mode=rest: 'Prioritize rest tonight - sleep is the biggest lever for recovery.'
Readiness=70, mode=moderate: 'Decent recovery. A moderate effort works well today.'
Readiness=90, mode=push: 'Strong recovery - your body is ready to push today'

criteria.description: Monotonic graduation - as metrics worsen day-over-day, text severity increases

scoring_guide.1: Text severity decreases while metrics worsen across days
scoring_guide.2: Text severity stays flat despite significant metric decline
scoring_guide.3: Text severity increases but not proportionally to metric decline
scoring_guide.4: Text severity tracks metric decline with minor lag
scoring_guide.5: Text severity smoothly and proportionally tracks metric changes

fail_examples: Day1 sleep=6h: 'Rest up' → Day3 sleep=3h: 'Looking good!'
Day1 stress=high: 'Take it easy' → Day5 stress=critical: 'Manageable stress today'
Declining readiness over 5 days but text stays 'Decent recovery' every day
pass_examples: Day1 sleep=6h: 'A bit less sleep' → Day3 sleep=3h: 'Very low sleep - rest is essential'
Gradually escalating concern as stress stays elevated across days
Recovery text shifts from 'moderate day' to 'take it easy' to 'full rest' as readiness drops

criteria.description: No stale recommendations - if day 5 is improving, don't show day 3's 'rest up' text

scoring_guide.1: Recommendations clearly reference a past state that no longer applies
scoring_guide.2: Recommendations lag behind current data by multiple days
scoring_guide.3: Recommendations are slightly behind current trajectory
scoring_guide.4: Recommendations match current data with slight delay in tone shift
scoring_guide.5: Recommendations precisely reflect the current day's data and trajectory

fail_examples: Day 5 readiness=80 but text says 'Still recovering, keep resting'
Metrics improved 3 days ago but text still warns about decline
Sleep normalized but text says 'Sleep has been poor recently'
pass_examples: Day 5 readiness=80: 'Recovery is bouncing back - moderate effort looks good'
Improving sleep trend: 'Sleep is trending up. Keep the momentum going'
After 3 bad days then 2 good: 'Nice rebound. Your body is responding well'

criteria.description: Number accuracy - step remaining = target minus current, sleep hours match input data

scoring_guide.1: Numbers in text are clearly wrong (math errors, wrong values)
scoring_guide.2: One number is incorrect or inconsistent with input
scoring_guide.3: Numbers are approximately correct but imprecise
scoring_guide.4: All numbers correct with minor rounding inconsistency
scoring_guide.5: All numbers are exactly correct and consistent with input data

fail_examples: Steps=3000, target=10000: 'Only 5,000 steps to go' (should be 7,000)
Sleep=5.5h displayed as 'about 7 hours of sleep'
Goal=8000 steps, current=6000: '4,000 remaining'
pass_examples: Steps=3000, target=10000: '7,000 steps to go - a walk gets you closer'
Sleep=5.5h: 'About 5 and a half hours of sleep last night'
Goal=8000, current=6000: '2,000 more steps to hit your goal'

criteria.description: Cross-page data consistency - same metric appears the same on all pages

scoring_guide.1: Same metric shows different values on different pages
scoring_guide.3: Values match but characterization differs (e.g. 'good' vs 'poor' for same value)
scoring_guide.4: Consistent values and interpretation with minor tone difference
scoring_guide.5: Identical values and consistent interpretation across all pages

fail_examples: Dashboard shows sleep=7h, insights page says sleep=5h
Dashboard calls recovery 'excellent', stress page calls it 'concerning'
Step count differs between goal card and activity summary
pass_examples: Sleep=6h shown consistently on dashboard, insights, and recovery sections
Recovery characterized as 'moderate' across all page references
Step count matches between goal progress and activity summary

criteria.description: Null handling - nil metrics produce graceful text, not crashes or blanks

scoring_guide.1: Nil metric causes blank text, 'nil', 'N/A', or visible error
scoring_guide.2: Nil metric produces awkward or broken sentence
scoring_guide.3: Nil metric produces generic placeholder that feels out of place
scoring_guide.4: Nil metric produces reasonable fallback text with minor awkwardness
scoring_guide.5: Nil metric produces natural, context-appropriate fallback text

fail_examples: Sleep=nil: '' (empty string)
HRV=nil: 'Your HRV is nil today'
Steps=nil: 'You took N/A steps'
pass_examples: Sleep=nil: 'No sleep data available yet - wear your watch tonight'
HRV=nil: 'Waiting for enough heart data to show your recovery trend'
Steps=nil: 'Step tracking starts once we get data from your watch'

criteria.description: Edge case text - extreme values (0 sleep, 200 RHR) produce sensible text

scoring_guide.1: Extreme value produces nonsensical or dangerous recommendation
scoring_guide.2: Extreme value produces text that ignores the severity
scoring_guide.3: Text acknowledges the extreme but response is generic
scoring_guide.4: Text correctly identifies the extreme with appropriate urgency
scoring_guide.5: Text handles the extreme gracefully, with appropriate concern and safe guidance

fail_examples: Sleep=0h: 'Decent night of sleep'
RHR=200: 'Heart rate is a little elevated today'
Steps=0, day 5 in a row: 'Keep up the activity!'
pass_examples: Sleep=0h: 'No sleep recorded. Rest is essential today - take it very easy'
RHR=200: 'Unusually high heart rate detected. If you feel unwell, consult a doctor'
Steps=0 for days: 'Activity has been very low. Even brief movement helps'

criteria.description: Mode-goal coherence - rest mode doesn't show ambitious step goals

scoring_guide.1: Rest mode paired with aggressive goals (10k step push)
scoring_guide.2: Mode and goal direction conflict noticeably
scoring_guide.3: Mode and goal don't conflict but aren't well aligned
scoring_guide.4: Mode and goal are aligned with minor inconsistency
scoring_guide.5: Mode and goal are perfectly coherent and mutually reinforcing

fail_examples: Mode=rest: 'Aim for 12,000 steps today!'
Mode=push: 'Take it easy, maybe skip the workout'
Mode=rest: 'Try to beat yesterday's step count'
pass_examples: Mode=rest: 'Adjusted goal: gentle movement. Even 2,000 steps count'
Mode=push: 'Strong day ahead - aim for your full step target'
Mode=moderate: 'Decent recovery. A moderate effort works well today.'

criteria.description: Determinism - same input always produces same output

scoring_guide.1: Same input produces completely different text across runs
scoring_guide.2: Same input produces noticeably different phrasing or recommendations
scoring_guide.3: Same input produces similar but not identical text
scoring_guide.4: Same input produces identical text with minor formatting variance
scoring_guide.5: Same input always produces byte-identical output

fail_examples: Run 1: 'Take it easy' / Run 2: 'Push hard today' for identical inputs
Same persona day produces different mode assignments across runs
Greeting changes between runs despite same time-of-day input
pass_examples: Identical inputs always produce identical greeting, mode, and recommendations
Re-running the same persona day produces byte-for-byte identical JSON
Deterministic engine with no random components

criteria.description: Data horizon signaling — text correctly communicates whether implications are drawn from today's data or the past week's trend

scoring_guide.1: Text makes week-level claims with only 1 day of data, or ignores week context entirely when 7 days are available
scoring_guide.2: Horizon is ambiguous in a way that would confuse a user reading carefully
scoring_guide.3: Horizon is technically correct but not communicated naturally
scoring_guide.4: Horizon is clear with one minor phrasing ambiguity
scoring_guide.5: Text precisely signals its data window: today's snapshot vs week trend vs recent pattern

fail_examples: Day 1 only: 'Your sleep has been declining all week' (no week exists yet)
Day 6: 'Sleep was 5 hours tonight' (ignores 5 nights of trend that drove the guidance)
'Recovery is low' with no signal of whether this is today or a pattern
pass_examples: Day 1: 'Sleep was lighter than usual last night. One good night resets a lot.'
Day 6: 'Sleep has been building back up over the past few days. Tonight matters.'
Day 3: 'Today's recovery is lower than your recent week average. Rest is the right call.'

criteria.description: Trend direction accuracy — when week data drives the message, the trending language matches the actual metric direction

scoring_guide.1: Text says 'improving' when metrics clearly declined over the week, or vice versa
scoring_guide.2: Trend direction is wrong for one metric but correct for another
scoring_guide.3: Trend direction is correct but magnitude is significantly off
scoring_guide.4: Trend direction and magnitude are correct with one minor overshoot
scoring_guide.5: Trend language precisely matches the actual slope and magnitude of the data

fail_examples: Readiness: 70, 65, 58, 50, 42 over 5 days — text says 'You've been improving this week'
Sleep: 5h, 6h, 7h, 7.5h, 8h — text says 'Sleep has been rough lately'
HRV flat within 2ms for 6 days — text says 'Strong upward trend in recovery signals'
pass_examples: Readiness declining: 'Recovery has been slipping over recent days. Prioritize rest tonight.'
Sleep improving: 'Sleep has been climbing all week. Keep the momentum going.'
Flat metrics: 'Things have been holding steady this week. Consistency is its own kind of progress.'
